name: Agentic Multi-Channel Triage & CRM Ops — MOCK (no credentials required)

nodes.name: Mock Gmail Message

assignments.name: subject
assignments.value: Refund never processed, this is urgent

assignments.name: body
assignments.value: I was charged twice last week and still have no refund. This is extremely frustrating and I need this fixed today.

nodes.notes: Stands in for the real Gmail Trigger + Extract Email Fields nodes. Edit these values to try different scenarios.

nodes.name: Mock Telegram Message

assignments.name: body
assignments.value: Hey, what are your support hours?

nodes.notes: Stands in for the real Telegram Trigger + Extract Telegram Fields nodes.

nodes.name: Mock WhatsApp Message

assignments.name: body
assignments.value: Win a free prize now, click this link!!!

nodes.notes: Stands in for the real WhatsApp Trigger + Extract WhatsApp Fields nodes. Sample body is intentionally spam-like to demo that routing branch.

nodes.name: Normalized Inbound
nodes.notes: Same junction role as in the real workflow. Only one of the three Mock *Message nodes needs to be connected/run at a time -- by default all three feed in so you can pick which branch to execute.

nodes.name: Route by Category/Priority
nodes.notes: Identical logic to the real workflow's node of the same name -- no mocking needed here, it's pure conditional routing.

nodes.name: Route Reply by Channel
nodes.notes: Only reached for standard-priority messages, same as the real workflow.

parameters.jsCode: return $input.all().map((item) => ({ json: { mock_action: 'Would create Gmail draft reply', to: item.json.from, subject: 'Re: ' + item.json.subject, body: item.json.output.suggested_reply } }));
return $input.all().map((item) => ({ json: { mock_action: 'Would send Telegram reply', chatId: item.json.replyTarget, text: item.json.output.suggested_reply } }));
return $input.all().map((item) => ({ json: { mock_action: 'Would send WhatsApp reply', to: item.json.replyTarget, text: item.json.output.suggested_reply } }));
